'''TK-363''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="SWG:TDP" /> [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="CVD" /> [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] who lived during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="SWG:TDP" />

==Biography==
Sometime after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], TK-363 was stationed on [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]]. He guarded Storage room 02 in the lower section of the station.<ref name="SWG:TDP" />

{{GameDS}}
{{Quote|Very good. Feel the anger flowing through you. There's a stormtrooper on the lower level. Convince him to sabotage the station. It may kill him, but that's of no concern to me.|An unidentified darksider, to a fallen Jedi apprentice|<ref name="SWG:TDP" />}}
In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] who was following the training of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]]-in-exile [[Nos'lyn]] fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. The fallen Jedi apprentice later came under the tutelage of a mysterious [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]. As a test of faith in the dark side, the darksider ordered the spacer to use a [[Mind trick/Legends|mind trick]] to convince TK-363 to sabotage Tansarii Point Station. The apprentice persuaded the stormtrooper to destroy the station by going down to the [[Engine/Legends|engine]] room and overloading the [[Reactor/Legends|reactors]]. The spacer instructed him to wait until they had left the station before completing his duty. Nos'lyn admonished the spacer for doing this and not considering the consequences of their actions.<ref name="SWG:TDP" />

Around the same time, TK-363 met a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] working for [[Jake Felan]] in the lower section of Tansarii Point Station. The smuggler needed to dump a crate of sliced weapons from [[Station Gamma]] to the Storage room 02. However, the smuggler was not allowed to access the room. Instead of just shooting the stormtrooper, the smuggler lured the soldier and convinced him to let him access the room. The spacer dumped the weapons as planned and was paid 500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] by Felan for the work.<ref name="SWG:TotT">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Smuggler|Tricks of the Trade|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
TK-363 appeared as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:TDP" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], 2011.<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> TK-363 was added to the game as part of the "[[W:c:swg:New Player Tutorial|New Player Tutorial]]," introduced with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref>

The events mentioned above only occurred if the player took a dark-side approach to one of the first quests they received as the "Jedi" profession. Nos'lyn contacted the player later in the game from Tansarii Point Station, making it apparent that the mind trick was only temporary.<ref name="SWG:TDP" />
